name: marshmallow
what_it_does: Python object serialization/deserialization and validation library. marshmallow converts complex Python objects (ORM models, dataclasses) to/from Python dicts and validates input data against schemas. Predates Pydantic as the go-to Python serialization library; still widely used in Flask + SQLAlchemy ecosystems and applications that don't use Pydantic.
use_cases: Serialize SQLAlchemy ORM models to JSON for agent API responses without Pydantic
Validate and deserialize incoming API request data in Flask agent backends
Convert complex Python objects to primitive dicts for agent serialization pipelines
Build reusable validation schemas for agent data ingestion with nested object support
Use flask-marshmallow for integrated Flask + SQLAlchemy + marshmallow agent API backends
not_for: New projects with FastAPI — Pydantic is the standard for FastAPI; marshmallow is Flask-era technology
Type-safe applications — Pydantic provides better type integration and IDE support
Performance-critical serialization — orjson + Pydantic is significantly faster
best_when: You're working with a Flask or older Python codebase that uses marshmallow, or you need fine-grained serialization control not available in Pydantic.
avoid_when: Starting a new FastAPI project — use Pydantic. marshmallow is legacy for modern async Python development.

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: ValidationError contains structured error dict with field names and error messages. Errors are per-field and well-organized for programmatic handling.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Deterministic serialization and validation from same input and schema.
known_agent_gotchas: marshmallow 3.x has breaking changes from 2.x — strict=True is now the default; error handling changed significantly
Schema.load() (deserialization) and Schema.dump() (serialization) operate differently — load validates, dump does not by default
Missing fields in input raise ValidationError only if required=True — otherwise missing fields return load_default silently
Nested schemas must be instantiated or referenced by string — nested objects in dicts require explicit Nested() field definition
marshmallow does not generate JSON Schema or OpenAPI specs automatically — use apispec or marshmallow-jsonschema for schema documentation
Many/collection serialization uses Schema(many=True) — forgetting many=True on a list input silently processes only the first item in some cases

security_score_components.security_notes: Local validation library — no network access. Validation logic is the security boundary — ensure schemas validate all agent inputs.
